hero.title: The solid foundation for agent-driven projects
hero.description: Purrfold scaffolds production-ready projects with quality gates, agent skills, and shadcn UI — so your agents build on solid ground from day one.

tabs.title: Quick start
tabs.agentPrompt: I want to scaffold a new Next.js app with purrfold. Run `npx purrfold@latest my-app --yes --unit --e2e --commitlint --mcp` to set up the project with quality gates, testing, and agent skills. After scaffolding, run `npm run check` to verify everything works.

skills.description: Curated agent skills for consistent, high-quality code.

install.title: Install Purrfold

output.agentIntro: Scaffold a new Next.js app with purrfold (https://www.npmjs.com/package/purrfold). Read the README or run `npx purrfold@latest info --json` to discover available options.
output.agentOutro: Run `npm run check` after scaffolding to verify everything works.

items.description: Include or skip Vitest + React Testing Library
Include or skip commitlint + Husky commit-msg hook
Forward extra arguments to shadcn init, including --preset <id>
Install shadcn MCP for Claude, Codex, and OpenCode
Fallback icon library for the home-page cat

preset.title: Using a shadcn preset
preset.description: Pass a preset ID to shadcn init to skip the interactive style selection:

quality.cta: Review the quality stack Purrfold installs so projects start with safe defaults and fast feedback.
quality.description: See why the baseline tooling ships by default and which optional tools appear when you opt into deeper testing.
quality.title: Quality signals

overview.title: Default quality stack
overview.description: Purrfold installs the baseline checks by default to catch regressions early and keep feedback close to the code.
overview.defaultNote: React Doctor and React Scan are part of the default stack. Vitest, commitlint, and Playwright only appear when their install choices are selected.

qualityGates.title: Quality gates
qualityGates.description: Default signals that catch issues before they spread.

commitHygiene.title: Commit hygiene
commitHygiene.description: Formatting and commit checks that keep changes clean.

runtimeInsight.title: Runtime insight
runtimeInsight.description: Visibility that helps confirm the app stays healthy in the browser.

eslint.name: ESLint
eslint.purpose: Linting that enforces real project rules: effect discipline, boolean prop naming, ordered imports, type-only imports, promise safety, and console output limited to warn/error.
eslint.whenHelp: When you want feedback that catches React, TypeScript, and import hygiene issues before review.

prettier.name: Prettier
prettier.whenHelp: When you want formatting to stay automatic and boring.

husky.name: Husky
husky.purpose: Git hooks that run local checks before commits.
husky.whenHelp: When you want commit-time guardrails without extra ceremony.

commitlint.name: commitlint
commitlint.purpose: Commit message validation for conventional commits.
commitlint.whenHelp: When commit hygiene is part of the install you chose.

react-doctor.name: React Doctor
react-doctor.purpose: React diagnostics for maintainability and performance.
react-doctor.whenHelp: When you want quality gates to surface issues before review.

react-scan.name: React Scan
react-scan.purpose: Runtime scanning that highlights rendering problems.
react-scan.whenHelp: When browser behavior needs an extra health check.

vitest.name: Vitest
vitest.purpose: Fast unit tests for code that needs repeatable assertions.
vitest.whenHelp: When you enabled unit testing during installation.

playwright.name: Playwright
playwright.purpose: End-to-end tests for user flows that need browser coverage.
playwright.whenHelp: When you enabled E2E testing during installation.

typescript.name: TypeScript
typescript.purpose: Static typing that catches mistakes before runtime.
typescript.whenHelp: When type safety is the fastest way to prevent regressions.

skills.cta: Use this route to frame how the current landing connects operator guidance with reusable skills.
skills.description: Review the skills layer that supports installation, verification, and future localized expansion.
skills.title: Skill surface

overview.title: Skills with equal weight
overview.description: Each skill solves a different problem. This page shows where it fits, what it helps with, and a short example of its utility.

vercel-composition-patterns.name: Vercel Composition Patterns
vercel-composition-patterns.purpose: React composition patterns for flexible, maintainable component APIs.
vercel-composition-patterns.whenHelp: When building reusable components or refactoring components with boolean prop proliferation.
vercel-composition-patterns.example: Example: split a card into Card, CardHeader, and CardBody instead of adding boolean props.

project-architecture.name: Project Architecture
project-architecture.purpose: Architecture guardrails for UI, layout, state flow, theming, and server/client boundaries.
project-architecture.whenHelp: When designing component structure, state management, or deciding server vs client boundaries.
project-architecture.example: Example: decide whether a page stays server-first or needs a client island.

architecture-decision-records.name: Architecture Decision Records
architecture-decision-records.purpose: Document significant technical decisions with context, tradeoffs, and rationale.
architecture-decision-records.whenHelp: When making a non-trivial architectural choice that future agents should understand.
architecture-decision-records.example: Example: capture why you chose server actions over route handlers.

decision-doc-sync.name: Decision Doc Sync
decision-doc-sync.purpose: Keep documentation synchronized when architecture, UI, or conventions change.
decision-doc-sync.whenHelp: After structural changes that affect docs, ADRs, or quality gates.
decision-doc-sync.example: Example: update the ADR when a component boundary changes.

next-best-practices.name: Next.js Best Practices
next-best-practices.purpose: Next.js file conventions, RSC boundaries, data patterns, async APIs, and optimization.
next-best-practices.whenHelp: When building pages, layouts, routes, or data fetching in the App Router.
next-best-practices.example: Example: keep route components server-first and await async params.

vercel-react-best-practices.name: Vercel React Best Practices
vercel-react-best-practices.purpose: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines.
vercel-react-best-practices.whenHelp: When optimizing rendering, bundle size, or data loading patterns.
vercel-react-best-practices.example: Example: memoize only when profiling shows a real rendering cost.

shadcn.name: shadcn/ui
shadcn.purpose: Manages shadcn components, styling, presets, and project configuration.
shadcn.whenHelp: When adding, customizing, or troubleshooting shadcn components.
shadcn.example: Example: compose a form from shadcn primitives before writing custom UI.

vitest.name: Vitest
vitest.purpose: Testing patterns for unit and integration tests with Vitest and Testing Library.
vitest.whenHelp: When writing or debugging Vitest tests, mocking, or snapshot testing.
vitest.example: Example: test async UI behavior with await and precise assertions.

playwright-best-practices.name: Playwright Best Practices
playwright-best-practices.purpose: Comprehensive E2E testing patterns with Playwright.
playwright-best-practices.whenHelp: When writing E2E tests, debugging flaky tests, or setting up test infrastructure.
playwright-best-practices.example: Example: cover a locale switch with stable locators and assertions.

playwright-cli.name: Playwright CLI
playwright-cli.purpose: Browser automation and CLI-driven web testing.
playwright-cli.whenHelp: When automating browser interactions or debugging Playwright tests.
playwright-cli.example: Example: reproduce a bug by running a focused browser flow from the CLI.

react-doctor.name: React Doctor
react-doctor.whenHelp: Before commits to catch maintainability issues early.
react-doctor.example: Example: scan a page before commit to catch unnecessary client work.

verification-before-completion.name: Verification Before Completion
verification-before-completion.purpose: Run verification commands before claiming work is complete.
verification-before-completion.whenHelp: When finishing a task — ensures evidence matches claims.
verification-before-completion.example: Example: run the verification command before saying the fix is done.

project-min-evaluation.name: Project Minimum Evaluation
project-min-evaluation.purpose: Run minimum local quality checks before marking implementation complete.
project-min-evaluation.whenHelp: Before marking a feature as done — runs lint, typecheck, format, tests.
project-min-evaluation.example: Example: run lint, typecheck, format, and tests before closing a task.

systematic-debugging.name: Systematic Debugging
systematic-debugging.purpose: Structured methodology for debugging bugs and test failures.
systematic-debugging.whenHelp: When encountering unexpected behavior, test failures, or elusive bugs.
systematic-debugging.example: Example: reduce a failing test by isolating one assumption at a time.

typescript-advanced-types.name: Advanced TypeScript Types
typescript-advanced-types.purpose: Advanced type system features for type-safe applications.
typescript-advanced-types.whenHelp: When implementing complex type logic or reusable type utilities.
typescript-advanced-types.example: Example: model a discriminated union for a skill entry.
